title: Rumble Closes Acquisition of Northern Data
author: Not Specified
description: Rumble (NASDAQ: RUM) has completed its acquisition of Northern Data AG, gaining approximately 85.2% ownership. The deal provides Rumble with over 200 MW of unmonetized energy capacity, roughly 22,000 NVIDIA GPUs, and access to one of Europe's largest independent GPU estates. Northern Data raised its 2026 revenue outlook to 170-190 million euros from 130-150 million euros, with GPU utilization reaching 85%. Rumble also announced a $270 million multi-year contract with Together AI for GPU cloud capacity.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Acquisition significantly expands Rumble's infrastructure capabilities with 200+ MW of unmonetized power capacity, 22,000 high-end NVIDIA GPUs, and a $270 million validated contract with Together AI. Northern Data's revenue outlook increased 30%, demonstrating strong demand and immediate financial uplift to the combined entity.

Acquisition significantly expands Rumble's infrastructure capabilities with 200+ MW of unmonetized power capacity, 22,000 high-end NVIDIA GPUs, and a $270 million validated contract with Together AI. Northern Data's revenue outlook increased 30%, demonstrating strong demand and immediate financial uplift to the combined entity.

Indirect positive sentiment as the primary beneficiary of the deal through deployment of approximately 22,000 H100/H200 GPUs and future Blackwell B300 systems, validating strong demand for NVIDIA's high-end computing hardware in the AI infrastructure market.

title: Rumble Announces Final Results of Exchange Offer for Northern Data
author: Rumble Inc.
description: Rumble Inc. has successfully secured approximately 85.2% of Northern Data AG's outstanding shares through its exchange offer, with 8,174,379 shares tendered representing 46.2% of non-committed shares. The additional acceptance period expired on June 1, 2026, and closing is expected in mid-June 2026. All regulatory approvals have been received. The combined entity aims to become a leading independent force in AI computation, cloud infrastructure, and digital video innovation.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Rumble successfully achieved 85.2% shareholder acceptance for the Northern Data acquisition, exceeding typical thresholds. All regulatory approvals obtained, closing expected mid-June 2026. The transaction demonstrates strong strategic positioning in AI and cloud infrastructure markets with compelling synergy potential.

title: Rumble Exchange Offer for Northern Data will Expire on June 1, 2026 – Best and Final Offer for Shareholders to Tender Shares
author: Rumble Inc.
description: Rumble Inc. announced that its exchange offer to acquire Northern Data AG will expire on June 1, 2026, with no extension. Northern Data shareholders can exchange each share for 2.0281 newly issued Rumble Class A common shares. All regulatory approvals have been received, and both companies' boards unanimously recommend shareholders accept the offer. The transaction is expected to close mid-June 2026.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Rumble is acquiring Northern Data to expand its AI and high-performance computing capabilities. The deal has received all regulatory approvals, boards recommend acceptance, and closing is on track for mid-June 2026, indicating strong deal momentum and strategic growth.

Rumble is acquiring Northern Data to expand its AI and high-performance computing capabilities. The deal has received all regulatory approvals, boards recommend acceptance, and closing is on track for mid-June 2026, indicating strong deal momentum and strategic growth.

title: RUM Investors Have Opportunity to Join Rumble Inc. Fraud Investigation with the Schall Law Firm
author: Schall Law Firm
description: The Schall Law Firm announced an investigation into Rumble Inc. (NASDAQ: RUM) for potential securities law violations following the company's Q1 2026 earnings report on May 14, 2026. Rumble disclosed significantly higher net losses due to increased marketing costs and R&D spending, resulting in an 11.9% stock price decline. The firm is seeking shareholders who suffered losses to participate in potential litigation.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company faces a securities investigation for allegedly issuing false or misleading statements regarding financial performance. Q1 2026 results showed significantly larger net losses than expected due to higher marketing and R&D costs, leading to an 11.9% stock price decline and triggering shareholder litigation.

title: Rumble RUM Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript
author: Motley Fool Transcribing
description: Rumble reported Q1 2026 revenue of $25.5 million (7% YoY growth) with 56 million monthly active users. The company secured 81% of Northern Data shares with regulatory approvals complete, targeting June transaction close. Pro forma combined revenue would have been ~$75 million. Rumble Shorts set new records with 2 million daily views and is expected to monetize in H2 2026. The company ended the quarter with $233.4 million in liquidity and is scaling a $100 million Tether advertising commitment.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company demonstrated sequential user growth to 56M MAUs, strong progress on Northern Data acquisition with regulatory approvals complete, successful Rumble Shorts momentum (2M daily views), and strategic cloud infrastructure positioning. However, net loss widened to $30.3M and operating cash burn was $16.6M, partially offsetting positive developments.
